real_insight: When you and your team have run the analysis and still cannot recommend either path, the symmetry of dread itself is the diagnostic — the frame is the bug, not the conviction; do not respond by forcing a tactical decision inside a broken frame.
objection: I went looking for 'embrace the journey of decision-making' and did not find it. The verse explicitly distinguishes itself from refusal-to-decide: 'Engineers who use 2.6 as cover for refusing to make 50/50 calls have inverted it. Real equipoise commits.' That counter-example is the specific guard a real staff engineer needs — the FRAME-BUG state versus the avoidance state. The concrete scenario (Django monolith vs. Kubernetes service split, two quarters of headcount, compliance deadline, six-page Notion doc, three architecture reviews) is operationally specific enough that I can map it to two real situations from the last year. The implication 'name the state explicitly to the next level up; the correct intervention is reframing, not forcing a choice' is a real engineering protocol move that I have actually seen work — it is the difference between 'help me decide' and 'I cannot decide because the question itself is wrong.' Real insight, real protocol, real failure mode.
